lks2.objective: Defend positively, win the ball and make good decisions when transitioning from defending to attacking.
successCriteria: Attempts to win the ball fairly
Looks to score after winning the ball

uks2.objective: Develop 1v1 defending skills, recognising when to press, delay and tackle successfully.

space.description: Create a rectangular playing area with two small goals at one end. Most players start with a football. Three defenders begin without a football and attempt to win possession.

value.name: Responsibility
value.text: Good defenders take responsibility for their role, work hard to regain possession and react positively when things do not go their way.

steps: Set up a playing area with two small goals.
Most players begin with a football and dribble around the area.
Three Lions or Lionesses start as defenders without a football.
Defenders attempt to win possession from attackers.
When a defender wins the ball they immediately attack either goal.
The attacker whose ball was stolen becomes a new defender.
progressions: Limit the number of touches attackers can take.
Award extra points for defenders who score quickly after winning possession.
Add a second defender to create 2v1 defending situations.
Require attackers to use a specific turn or skill before defenders can challenge.
questions: What helps you win the ball fairly?
When is the best moment to tackle?
What should you do immediately after winning possession?

step.space: Reduce the playing area to increase defensive pressure or increase it to give attackers more success.
step.task: Defenders must score within five seconds of winning the ball.
step.equipment: Increase or decrease the size of the goals.
step.people: Adjust the number of defenders to make the challenge easier or harder.

base.showmanship: Celebrate successful defending as much as goals. Build excitement around becoming one of the Three Lions or Lionesses and praise brave attempts to regain possession.
